description: Merge conflict failed at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/polish-shadcn-builder/S3Vector-83b6. Agent: polish-shadcn-builder. Conflicting files: src/frontend/src/components/BackendSelector.tsx, src/frontend/src/components/SearchInterface.tsx.

name: mulch-config-domain-merge
description: When merging branches with mulch.config.yaml domain conflicts, combine all domains from both branches rather than choosing one

name: modular-multimodal-vector-infrastructure
description: Terraform modules for configurable multi-backend multimodal vector platform with 3 embedding provider types, 6+ vector stores, 4 modalities, cost estimation, and benchmark orchestration. Supports 216 configurations across deployment profiles from -800/mo with phased migration path.

content: When analyzing dual FastAPI layers, verify actual usage by checking run_api.py imports (src.api.main:app vs src.backend.main:app). Use grep to confirm zero references: 'rg "from src\.backend\.main import" --type py'. Document findings with line counts and usage context before proposing removal.

name: multi-modal-embedding-abstraction
description: Multi-modal platform design: Create ModalityType enum (TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO, VIDEO, MULTIMODAL), abstract EmbeddingProvider base class, and EmbeddingProviderFactory for registration. Each provider declares supported_modalities and implements generate_embeddings(). Enables side-by-side provider comparison and dynamic provider selection per modality.

name: sequential-merge-rebase-workflow
description: MERGE WORKFLOW: When merging multiple agent branches sequentially, REBASE remaining branches onto updated master after each merge. Without this, later branches conflict on shared files (.mulch/mulch.config.yaml, .seeds/issues.jsonl, etc.). The sequence is: merge A → rebase B onto master → merge B → rebase C onto master → merge C. Never force-delete unmerged branches that contain unique content — verify files are on master first. Lost docs/plans/EMBEDDING_MODELS_PLAN.md (506 lines) by force-deleting its branch before cherry-picking.

content: COORDINATOR MERGE PROTOCOL: 1) Collect all merge_ready signals. 2) Sort by dependency order. 3) Merge first branch. 4) For each remaining branch: spawn a quick lead to rebase it onto updated master, wait for completion, then merge. 5) Never force-clean worktrees with unmerged content until verified on master. 6) If ov merge fails, spawn a lead to resolve — coordinator cannot run git merge/checkout/rebase directly due to hooks.

title: Keep Tailwind v3.4 over v4
rationale: Tailwind v4 is a major breaking change (CSS-first config vs JS config) not worth the risk for a config update task. Frontend deps were already current (React 19, Vite 7, TanStack v5, TS 5.9).

content: LEAD AGENT SOP: Before spawning ANY builders, leads MUST complete a research/context phase: 1) Read all relevant plan docs and specs 2) Scout the actual codebase files they'll be modifying (read key files, understand patterns) 3) Web research if the task involves external APIs, new libraries, or AWS services 4) Document findings in a brief spec or dispatch context 5) ONLY THEN spawn builders with detailed, informed specs that include file paths, existing patterns to follow, and pitfalls to avoid. Leads that skip research produce builders that work blind and create inconsistent code. The lead's deep context is what makes builder output coherent.

CROSS-WORKSTREAM COLLABORATION: Leads working on the same project but different workstreams should be aware of each other's work. Coordinator should: 1) Include context about parallel workstreams in dispatch mail (what other leads are doing, shared interfaces/contracts) 2) Leads should send status updates that mention dependencies or interfaces they're creating that other workstreams need 3) When a lead creates an API contract, interface, or shared type that another workstream will consume, it should mail the coordinator who relays to the dependent lead 4) Avoid siloed builders — each builder should know WHERE its output fits in the larger system.

name: lead-context-building-pattern
description: LEAD CONTEXT BUILDING: Leads should use one of these strategies before spawning builders: A) Self-research: Read plan docs + key source files directly (if hooks allow) B) Scout delegation: Spawn 1-2 scouts to explore specific areas, wait for findings, then design builder specs from scout results C) Web research: Use available search tools for external API docs, library versions, AWS service details. The goal is that the lead's dispatch mail to builders contains: exact file paths, existing code patterns to follow, interface contracts, edge cases, and acceptance criteria derived from ACTUAL codebase understanding — not guesses from the plan doc alone.

name: config-backward-compatibility
description: When removing deprecated config systems, update package __init__.py with backward-compatibility aliases to prevent breaking existing imports (e.g., AppConfig = UnifiedConfiguration)

name: async-blocking-io-fix
description: Wrap blocking I/O calls (boto3, requests) in async functions with asyncio.to_thread() to prevent blocking the event loop. Applied to health check endpoint with 3 blocking calls

Force-deleted worktrees with unmerged unique content (root-builder: 44 files/3572 insertions Terraform, embedding-plan-writer: 1652 lines, scripts-docs-builder: start.sh/README). All work lost because 'ov worktree clean --completed --force' deletes ALL completed agent worktrees including those queued for rebase.
resolution: Never use --force on worktree clean while branches are pending rebase/merge. Instead: 1) Only force-clean explicitly discarded branches by name 2) Keep branches queued for rebase 3) List unmerged branches and verify each before force-cleaning 4) If branches must be preserved, merge or cherry-pick BEFORE cleaning

content: Terraform module structure: Each infrastructure module contains main.tf (resources), variables.tf (inputs with defaults), outputs.tf (exports), and optional lambda/ subdirectory for initialization functions. Modules use data.archive_file for Lambda packaging and null_resource with local-exec for first-deployment initialization.

name: terraform-multimodal-embedding-modules
description: Terraform multimodal embedding infrastructure: 3 embedding provider modules (bedrock_native IAM-only, marketplace/sagemaker with SageMaker endpoints + auto-scaling), pgvector_aurora module (Aurora Serverless v2 + Lambda initialization), sample_datasets module (S3 + Lambda population for 4 modalities), cost_estimator module (Lambda + API Gateway for real-time pricing). All modules follow opt-in pattern with comprehensive variables/outputs.

name: terraform-multimodal-infrastructure
description: Terraform modules for configurable multi-backend multimodal vector platform with 3 embedding providers (Bedrock native, AWS Marketplace, SageMaker custom), pgvector Aurora vector store, sample datasets module, and cost estimator API. All modules follow opt-in design with conditional count-based instantiation, Lambda initialization patterns for extensions/datasets, CloudWatch monitoring, and proper IAM role separation. Includes 4 deployment profiles (fast-start, comparison, full-multimodal, production) for different use cases.

content: Lambda initialization pattern for Terraform modules: Use archive_file data source to package Lambda code, invoke via null_resource provisioner on first apply. Store credentials in Secrets Manager, implement proper VPC configuration for database access, and use CloudWatch log groups for observability. Applied in pgvector (extension init) and sample_datasets (population) modules.

name: embedding-model-documentation
description: Document 27 models across 4 provider types (Bedrock, SageMaker Marketplace, JumpStart, External APIs) with unified abstraction layer, include API patterns, cost per 1K tokens, dimensions, use cases, and performance benchmarks for model selection guidance

name: multi-modal-embedding-provider-abstraction
description: Multi-modal embedding provider abstraction: ModalityType enum (TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO, VIDEO, MULTIMODAL) with EmbeddingProvider base class, factory auto-registration via @register_embedding_provider decorator, and async/await pattern using asyncio.to_thread for blocking boto3 calls. Implemented BedrockMultiModalProvider (Titan, Cohere, Nova, Marengo) and SageMakerEmbeddingProvider (Voyage, Jina, BGE, E5) with unified EmbeddingRequest/Response format.

content: REBASE-MERGE FLOW: When merging multiple branches sequentially, ALWAYS rebase remaining branches onto updated master after each merge. Flow: merge A → rebase B onto master → merge B → rebase C onto master → merge C. Send rebase instructions to owning lead when ov merge fails on conflicts. Never attempt direct merge without rebase when prior branches have already landed.

DOCUMENTATION AGENT SOP: Every lead MUST spawn or designate a documentation agent (builder or dedicated doc-writer) to update relevant docs as work progresses. Documentation should cover: 1) Updated ARCHITECTURE.md sections for new/changed modules, 2) API docs for new endpoints, 3) README updates for user-facing changes, 4) Plan file status updates (mark sections complete). This ensures other agents following the investigate→analyze→architect→implement SOP can find current documentation rather than stale plans.

INTELLIGENT REBASE-MERGE RESOLUTION: When rebasing a branch onto master and conflicts arise, NEVER simply discard one side. Instead: 1) For .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl files — APPEND both sides (each line is an independent record, combine all). 2) For .seeds/issues.jsonl — combine entries from both sides. 3) For source code with overlapping implementations — keep the more complete version as the base, then manually port any UNIQUE additions from the other side (new methods, new imports, new test cases). 4) For __init__.py and config files — merge all entries from both sides. The goal is zero information loss. Cherry-picking = information loss. Proper rebase resolution = combining the best of both sides.

name: multi-modal-embedding-provider
description: Multi-modal embedding provider pattern: Abstract base class (EmbeddingProvider) with factory auto-registration via @register_embedding_provider decorator. Supports TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO, VIDEO, MULTIMODAL modalities. Three provider types: BedrockMultiModalProvider (AWS native), SageMakerEmbeddingProvider (custom endpoints), ExternalEmbeddingProvider (OpenAI/Cohere APIs). Providers auto-register on import, enabling dynamic discovery via EmbeddingProviderFactory.get_provider_for_modality().

name: unified-ingestion-pipeline
description: Unified ingestion service orchestrates full pipeline: content → embeddings → vector storage. UnifiedIngestionService auto-selects embedding provider based on modality, generates embeddings, and stores to target vector store. Supports batch processing with configurable concurrency. Returns IngestionResult with timing breakdowns (embedding_time_ms, storage_time_ms) and error tracking.

content: Async provider implementations wrap blocking boto3 calls with asyncio.to_thread() to prevent event loop blocking. Pattern: response = await asyncio.to_thread(self.bedrock_runtime.invoke_model, ...). Applied to all AWS SDK calls (Bedrock, SageMaker Runtime) in embedding and vector store providers.

name: provider-tdd-stubs
description: TDD approach for provider interfaces: Write interface contract tests BEFORE implementation lands. Tests verify ABC structure, required methods, factory pattern, and config validation. Mark unimplemented tests with @pytest.mark.skip and TODO comments for Wave 2. Import errors are expected until implementation completes. Example: test_provider_interfaces.py tests VectorStoreProvider ABC and stubs EmbeddingProvider for future work.

name: react-real-time-polling
description: Real-time progress monitoring in React: Use useEffect with setInterval to poll API endpoints every 2s, update local state with progress data, render live charts with Recharts, and automatically redirect to results page on completion. Clean up interval in useEffect return. Suitable for long-running backend operations.

content: React page components convention: Create dedicated pages/ subdirectory under components/. Each page is a full-screen view with routing, independent state management, gradient background (bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100), max-width container (max-w-6xl/7xl), and consistent header with back navigation. Use shadcn/ui Card components for section grouping.

name: multi-step-wizard-flow
description: Multi-step wizard pattern: Configure → Review → Progress flow with localStorage persistence for configuration state, allowing users to navigate back to earlier steps. Each page handles distinct phase: input collection, confirmation, real-time monitoring.

name: deployment-polling-pattern
description: Deployment monitoring pattern: Poll infrastructure status every 5 seconds via API, update UI based on status, cleanup interval on unmount or completion. Success/failure states navigate to different UX paths.

name: react-page-composition
description: React page component structure: Build pages that compose existing components (SearchInterface, ResultsGrid, VideoPlayer, etc.) rather than duplicating logic. Pages handle routing, state coordination, and API integration. Use shadcn/ui Card/CardHeader/CardContent for layout consistency.

name: infrastructure-dashboard-pattern
description: Infrastructure dashboard: Combine real-time status polling (30s interval), operation modal (Terraform logs), and metrics display. Use mock data enrichment for demo purposes (queries_24h, avg_latency_ms) when API doesn't provide full metrics.

name: multi-page-workflow-routing
description: Multi-page workflow routing: Group related routes under common path prefixes (/deployment/*, /benchmark/*, /demo/*) with comments. Use hub pages as entry points (BenchmarkHubPage at /benchmark) with sub-routes for steps. Dynamic :id parameters enable content-specific pages.

name: terraform-validation-testing
description: Terraform validation test approach: Use pytest with parametrize for profile coverage, validate without backend init, mark AWS-dependent tests with requires_aws marker, verify module structure separately from config validation. Tests terraform syntax and configuration without requiring credentials.

name: comprehensive-test-suite-structure
description: Organized test directory: tests/{unit,integration,providers,e2e,terraform}/ with minimal mocking - only mock external network boundaries, use real FastAPI TestClient and service wiring

name: multi-modal-embedding-architecture
description: Platform uses EmbeddingProvider abstraction with ModalityType enum (TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO, VIDEO, MULTIMODAL), EmbeddingProviderFactory with decorator-based auto-registration, and UnifiedIngestionService orchestrating content→embeddings→vector storage pipeline. Providers: BedrockMultiModalProvider, SageMakerEmbeddingProvider, ExternalEmbeddingProvider (OpenAI, Cohere). Key endpoints: GET /api/embeddings/providers, GET /providers/{id}, POST /generate, GET /resources/vector-stores/comparison.

content: Terraform security: Never use hardcoded secrets in variables.tf defaults. Set default to empty string and require explicit value at deployment. Mark as sensitive = true.
S3 encryption: Always add aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration resource for S3 buckets. Use AES256 algorithm with bucket_key_enabled = true for cost optimization.

name: terraform-iam-scoping
description: Terraform IAM security hardening: Scope wildcard resources using data sources (aws_caller_identity, aws_region) for dynamic ARN construction. Replace s3:::*/* with arn:aws:s3:::${project_name}-*/*. Replace bedrock:*:*:model/* with arn:aws:bedrock:${region}:${account}:model/*. Use project-specific resource patterns over account-wide wildcards.

name: terraform-managed-policy-replacement
description: Terraform managed policy replacement: Replace overly permissive AWS managed policies (AmazonSageMakerFullAccess) with scoped custom policies. Use aws_iam_role_policy resource with minimal required actions (InvokeEndpoint, DescribeEndpoint) scoped to project resources.

name: terraform-conditional-resource-refs
description: Terraform conditional resource references: When module references another optional module output (e.g., module.s3vector[0].vector_bucket_name), add dependency check to count condition. Use count = var.deploy_feature && var.deploy_dependency ? 1 : 0 to prevent reference errors.

content: React Router v6 layout pattern: Use Outlet component in layout templates, not manual children prop. Parent Route renders <MainLayout />, child routes render page components directly without wrapping. This prevents double-rendering of layout components.

name: react-router-parent-redirects
description: React Router parent route redirects: Use Navigate component with replace prop to redirect parent paths to default child routes (e.g., /deployment → /deployment/configure, /demo → /demo/search). Preserves clean navigation structure while ensuring all links work.

content: React Context API pattern: Components should use context hooks (useBenchmark, useInfrastructure, useSearch) instead of calling api.* directly. This centralizes state management, enables automatic caching/invalidation, and simplifies components.

React Context mutation race conditions: Avoid setTimeout in mutation onSettled handlers for state cleanup - creates race conditions if new operations start before timeout. Use useEffect monitoring poll state or operation completion instead for proper cleanup timing.

name: embedding-provider-test-mocking
description: Mock AWS SageMaker/Bedrock responses with dict containing Mock object for 'Body' key (response['Body'].read()) rather than subscriptable Mock, wrap boto3 calls with asyncio.to_thread()

content: Adding new embedding models requires: 1) Add EmbeddingModelInfo to provider MODELS dict with dimensions/context/modality, 2) Add routing logic in generate_embedding, 3) Implement model-specific handler method with asyncio.to_thread wrapper

Async wrapper pattern for FastAPI routes: Wrap all blocking I/O (time.sleep, requests.*, boto3 calls) with asyncio.to_thread() or await asyncio.sleep() when called from async route handlers. Convert service methods to async if they contain blocking calls and are invoked from async contexts.
Route consolidation: Maintain single routers/ directory for all FastAPI route modules, not dual routes/ and routers/ directories. Keep route organization flat under src/api/routers/.

Vector store provider registration: Use centralized registration in vector_store_manager.py imports section. Individual provider files should NOT self-register via VectorStoreProviderFactory.register_provider() calls at module level to avoid double registration.

React deployment pages (DeploymentReviewPage) use InfrastructureContext hook instead of direct API calls for deployment operations. Context provides centralized state management, operation tracking (operationInProgress), and consistent error handling.

Sequential rebase-merge execution: When merging multiple branches in sequence (async-wrapper then polish-wizard), merge first branch, then immediately merge second. If second was already rebased onto the correct base, no additional rebase needed. Both branches merged cleanly (Tier 1): async-wrapper via fast-forward, polish-wizard via auto-merge with no conflicts. The .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl files combined correctly via git's auto-merge.
Toaster component should only render once at app root level (main.tsx), not in layout components. Multiple Toaster instances cause duplicate toast notifications.
Pages rendered inside MainLayout should not override layout background with min-h-screen or custom gradients. Let MainLayout handle page-level styling for consistency across all routes.

Wrap App component with ErrorBoundary at the root level to catch and gracefully handle React rendering errors globally. ErrorBoundary should show user-friendly error UI with retry/reload options and detailed stack traces in development mode only.
